2605.19285.title: Are Rationales Necessary and Sufficient? Tuning LLMs for Explainable Misinformation Detection
2605.19285.paper_summary: The rapid spread of misinformation on social media platforms has become a formidable challenge. To mitigate its proliferation, Misinformation Detection (MD) has emerged as a critical research topic. Traditional MD approaches based on small models typically perform binary classification through a black-box process. Recently, the rise of Large Language Models (LLMs) has enabled explainable MD, where models generate rationales that explain their decisions, thereby enhancing transparency. Existing explainable MD methods primarily focus on crafting sophisticated prompts to elicit rationales from off-the-shelf LLMs. In this work, we propose a pipeline to fine-tune a dedicated LLM specifically for explainable MD. Our pipeline begins by collecting large-scale fact-checked articles, and then uses multiple strong LLMs to produce veracity predictions and rationales. To ensure high-quality training data, we leverage a filtering strategy that selects only the correct instances for fine-tuning. While this pipeline is intuitive and prevalent, our experiments reveal that naive filtering based solely on label correctness is insufficient in practice and suffers from two critical limitations: (1) Coarse-grained labels cause insufficient rationales: Rationales filtered solely based on binary labels are insufficient to adequately support their decisions; (2) Over-verification behavior causes unnecessary rationales: Stronger LLMs tend to exhibit over-verification behavior, producing excessively verbose and unnecessary rationales. To address these issues, we introduce LONSREX, a novel data synthesis pipeline to Locate Necessary and Sufficient Rationales for Explainable MD. Specifically, we propose a metric that quantifies the contribution of each verification step to the final prediction, thereby evaluating its necessity and sufficiency. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of LONSREX.
2605.19285.paper_summary_zh: 社交媒體平台上虛假信息的迅速傳播已成為一個艱鉅的挑戰。為了減輕其擴散，虛假信息檢測（MD）已成為一個關鍵的研究主題。基於小型模型的傳統MD方法通常通過黑箱過程執行二元分類。最近，大型語言模型（LLMs）的興起使得可解釋的MD成為可能，這些模型生成解釋其決策的理由，從而增強了透明度。現有的可解釋MD方法主要集中在設計複雜的提示，以從現成的LLMs中引出理由。在這項工作中，我們提出了一個管道，以專門針對可解釋MD微調一個專用的LLM。我們的管道首先收集大規模的事實檢查文章，然後使用多個強大的LLM來生成真實性預測和理由。為了確保高質量的訓練數據，我們利用一種過濾策略，只選擇正確的實例進行微調。雖然這個管道直觀且普遍，但我們的實驗顯示，僅基於標籤正確性的天真過濾在實踐中是不足的，並且存在兩個關鍵限制：（1）粗粒度標籤導致理由不足：僅基於二元標籤過濾的理由不足以充分支持其決策；（2）過度驗證行為導致不必要的理由：較強的LLM往往會表現出過度驗證行為，生成過於冗長且不必要的理由。為了解決這些問題，我們引入了LONSREX，一個新穎的數據合成管道，用於定位可解釋MD所需的必要和充分理由。具體而言，我們提出了一種量化每個驗證步驟對最終預測貢獻的指標，從而評估其必要性和充分性。實驗結果證明了LONSREX的有效性。
2605.19285.author: Bing Wang et.al.
2605.19285.authors: Bing Wang, Rui Miao, Ximing Li, Chen Shen, Shaotian Yan, Changchun Li, Kaiyuan Liu, Xiaosong Yuan, Jieping Ye
